embeds.title: Kingdom of Kush
embeds.description:
The Kingdom of Kush (; Egyptian: 𓎡𓄿𓈙𓈉 kꜣš, Assyrian:  Kûsi, in LXX Χους or Αἰθιοπία; Coptic: ⲉϭⲱϣ Ecōš; Hebrew: כּוּשׁ Kūš), also known as the Kushite Empire, or simply Kush, was an ancient kingdom in Nubia, centered along the Nile Valley in what is now northern Sudan and southern Egypt.
The region

content: The "ten thousand years" is more of a metaphorical phrase than literal, though I believe Miryam has explicitly stated in her dialogue that she has been waiting for thousand(s) of years

Lemuria should predate all other events in the lore as far as I'm aware, the sinking of Lemuria was at minimum thousands of years prior, possibly tens of thousands of years prior

nah the thousands of years before all other civilizations tracks

but they're all universal in claiming it's way before even the ancient peoples like the philistines

Yep, confirmed in the original CN text that Uvhash's story occurs in the Kushi (Kuxi?) desert
I checked again just to make sure and yeah I don't see any real-world equivalents to it
it's just 库西 or kùxī
I can't find anything that could be remotely related to it so it's likely just a made up name
if there is a reference then I'm too uneducated to catch it
then again, thinking about it further and I think it's possible it could be referring to the Kush desert
since Londinium is London, Aglo Republic (thinking about it, probably more like Agro? as in like agriculture) is the USA, and Oakwood is, well, Hollywood
so Kushi could've been changed to Kuxi
just minor speculation though, I haven't read Uvhash's story yet so not sure if there's any actual definitive evidence

one of the characters in uvhash's story is a former mamluk (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mamluk) and they're highly associated with egypt/the kush desert area, so it would make sense for it to be kush

embeds.title: Mamluk
embeds.description: Mamluk or Mamaluk (; Arabic: مملوك, romanized: mamlūk (singular), مماليك, mamālīk (plural); translated as "one who is owned", meaning "slave") were non-Arab, ethnically diverse (mostly Turkic, Caucasian, Mongols, Eastern and Southeastern European) enslaved mercenaries, slave-soldiers, and freed slav
